snapshot.content:
# Specification: Notification System Expansion

## Overview
This document specifies the expansion of the Notification System to support multiple roles and events within the AI Chromosome App. It builds upon the existing `NotificationService` and `FirestoreListener` in the Backend, and the `AppHeader` in the Frontend.

## 1. Notification Matrix

| Event ID | Event Description | Trigger Condition | Recipient Role | Channel | Priority |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `ORDER_PENDING` | New Test Order created | `TestOrder.status == 'PENDING'` | **Lab Manager** | In-App | Medium |
| `ORDER_ASSIGNED` | Specialist assigned to order | `TestOrder.specialist_id` changed | **Lab Specialist** | Push + In-App | High |
| `ANALYSIS_READY` | Analysis waiting for review | `TestOrder.status == 'WAITING_APPROVAL'` | **Lab Manager** | In-App | High |
| `ORDER_COMPLETED` | Result approved & finalized | `TestOrder.status == 'COMPLETED'` | **Clinician** | Push + In-App | High |
| `ORDER_REJECTED` | Result rejected by Manager | `TestOrder.status == 'REJECTED'` | **Lab Specialist** | Push + In-App | High |
| `APPT_CANCELLED` | Appointment cancelled | `Appointment.status == 'CANCELLED'` | **Clinician** | In-App | Medium |

## 2. Data Structure (Firestore)

### 2.1. `notifications` Collection
Stored per user or globally with a recipient field. For MVP, we will use a global collection where each doc specifies the `recipient_id`.

```json
{
  "id": "string (uuid)",
  "recipient_id": "string (uid)",
  "title": "string",
  "body": "string",
  "type": "ORDER_PENDING | ORDER_ASSIGNED | ANALYSIS_READY | ORDER_COMPLETED | ORDER_REJECTED | APPT_CANCELLED",
  "related_id": "string (order_id or appointment_id)",
  "is_read": "boolean",
  "created_at": "timestamp",
  "data": {
    "patient_name": "string",
    "patient_code": "string"
  }
}
```

### 2.2. `audit_logs` (Existing Structure)
No changes required, but must ensure all notification triggers are logged.
```json
{
  "target_id": "order_id",
  "action_type": "STATUS_CHANGE | NOTIFICATION_SENT",
  "old_value": {"status": "..."},
  "new_value": {"status": "..."},
  "timestamp": "timestamp",
  "user_id": "uid"
}
```

## 3. Backend Implementation (Python)

### 3.1. `FirestoreListener.py`
- Expand the `on_snapshot` loop to detect the statuses in the matrix.
- Call corresponding `NotificationService` methods.

### 3.2. `NotificationService.py`
- Add specific methods for each event.
- Each method must:
  1. Construct the FCM payload.
  2. Call `send_to_user` (Push).
  3. Create a new document in the `notifications` Firestore collection (In-App).

## 4. Frontend Implementation (Flutter)

### 4.1. Notification Cubit
- A global Cubit that streams `notifications` where `recipient_id == current_user_uid`.
- Updates the badge count in `AppHeader`.

### 4.2. Deep Linking
- When a notification is clicked:
  - `ORDER_*`: Navigate to `TestOrderDetailPage(id: related_id)`.
  - `APPT_CANCELLED`: Navigate to `AppointmentDetailPage(id: related_id)`.

## 5. Acceptance Criteria (Verification)
- [ ] Backend detects `WAITING_APPROVAL` status and sends notification to Manager.
- [ ] Backend detects `COMPLETED` status and sends push notification to Clinician.
- [ ] Frontend displays unread count on the bell icon.
- [ ] Clicking a notification navigates to the correct detail screen.
snapshot.title: Notification System Expansion

{

changes.newValue:
# Specification: Notification System (UI-Only / Ephemeral)

## Overview
This document specifies a simplified, non-persistent notification system. Notifications are delivered via Push FCM and displayed in-app as temporary UI elements (Snackbars/Dialogs) using the existing `NotificationFactory`.

## 1. Notification Matrix (Trigger Only)
Events remain the same, but the delivery is purely ephemeral.

| Event ID | recipient Role | Channel | Priority |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `ORDER_PENDING` | **Lab Manager** | Push/UI | Medium |
| `ORDER_ASSIGNED` | **Lab Specialist** | Push/UI | High |
| `ANALYSIS_READY` | **Lab Manager** | Push/UI | High |
| `ORDER_COMPLETED` | **Clinician** | Push/UI | High |
| `ORDER_REJECTED` | **Lab Specialist** | Push/UI | High |

## 2. Data Strategy
- **Firestore**: No `notifications` collection will be created.
- **Audit Logs**: Status changes are still logged for system traceability, but NOT as user notifications.

## 3. Backend Implementation (Python)
- `NotificationService.py`: 
  - REMOVE `_save_notification`.
  - `send_to_user` and `send_to_role` will only invoke Firebase Messaging (FCM).

## 4. Frontend Implementation (Flutter)
- **FCM Listener**: Implement a foreground message handler in `main.dart` or a `NotificationCubit`.
- **UI Feedback**: When a message is received, call `NotificationFactory.show()` to display a Snackbar or Dialog based on the `type` payload.
- **Notification Bell**: (Optional) Could show a session-based badge, but will be cleared upon app restart.

## 5. Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backend sends FCM push when status changes.
- [ ] Frontend catches the foreground message.
- [ ] `NotificationFactory` renders the appropriate popup while the app is active.

# Specification: Notification System (UI-Only / Ephemeral)

## Overview
This document specifies a simplified, non-persistent notification system. Notifications are delivered via Push FCM and displayed in-app as temporary UI elements (Snackbars/Dialogs) using the existing `NotificationFactory`.

## 1. Notification Matrix (Trigger Only)
Events remain the same, but the delivery is purely ephemeral.

| Event ID | recipient Role | Channel | Priority |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `ORDER_PENDING` | **Lab Manager** | Push/UI | Medium |
| `ORDER_ASSIGNED` | **Lab Specialist** | Push/UI | High |
| `ANALYSIS_READY` | **Lab Manager** | Push/UI | High |
| `ORDER_COMPLETED` | **Clinician** | Push/UI | High |
| `ORDER_REJECTED` | **Lab Specialist** | Push/UI | High |

## 2. Data Strategy
- **Firestore**: No `notifications` collection will be created.
- **Audit Logs**: Status changes are still logged for system traceability, but NOT as user notifications.

## 3. Backend Implementation (Python)
- `NotificationService.py`: 
  - REMOVE `_save_notification`.
  - `send_to_user` and `send_to_role` will only invoke Firebase Messaging (FCM).

## 4. Frontend Implementation (Flutter)
- **FCM Listener**: Implement a foreground message handler in `main.dart` or a `NotificationCubit`.
- **UI Feedback**: When a message is received, call `NotificationFactory.show()` to display a Snackbar or Dialog based on the `type` payload.
- **Notification Bell**: (Optional) Could show a session-based badge, but will be cleared upon app restart.

## 5. Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backend sends FCM push when status changes.
- [ ] Frontend catches the foreground message.
- [ ] `NotificationFactory` renders the appropriate popup while the app is active.
